demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged care facility; nursing staff present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I feel incredibly dizzy, my hands are shaking, and I think the nurse gave me my morning pills instead of my night pills.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The evening was meant to be a routine check of my diabetes and blood pressure, but I've come over very shaky and lightheaded after a mix-up with my medications tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: started a short time after the evening medication round tonight
symptom_narrative.duration: the last little while and I still feel awful
symptom_narrative.character: dizzy and wobbly, hands trembling, sweating a lot, and my heart is pounding
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: bad enough that I felt like I might faint and I can't get up
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all over — my head is swimming and my hands won't stop shaking
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on fairly suddenly and hasn't settled
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting still on the bed helps a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to sit up or stand makes the dizziness much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: cold sweat and a racing, thumping heartbeat; feeling anxious about the pill mix-up
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've felt shaky and sweaty like this once or twice before when my sugars have gone low
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I normally get about with my walking frame but right now I'm stuck on the bed, too dizzy to stand

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: type 2 diabetes that the staff manage with my insulin
past_medical_history.how_managed: insulin injections and tablets, monitored by the facility nurses
a blood pressure tablet every day

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: insulin injections (a fast one and a long overnight one)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged care facility; the nurse who did tonight's medication round is new; the staff have told me there was a mix-up with which insulin I was given tonight

communication_style.typical_message_style: frail elderly resident, a little frightened by the medication error, staff helping to relay information

objective_data_offered.value: she looks pale and sweaty, hands visibly trembling, sitting propped on the bed and unable to stand
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: nurse's visual description over the phone, not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: my heart feels like it's racing and pounding
the facility medication chart has been reviewed by the nurse and the medication mix-up from tonight's round has been documented before the call
